title: Leakage channels beyond distance
text: core diagnostic: signed Spatial Leakage Index.
code: set.seed(1) nc <- 8; ce <- cbind(runif(nc, .1, .9), runif(nc, .1, .9)); cl <- sample(nc, 400, TRUE) xy <- ce[cl, ] + cbind(rnorm(400, 0, .04), rnorm(400, 0, .04)) d <- data.frame(x = pmin(pmax(xy[, 1], 0), 1), y = pmin(pmax(xy[, 2], 0), 1)) d$z <- sin(2 * pi * d$x) + cos(2 * pi * d$y) + rnorm(400, 0, .15) grid <- as.matrix(expand.grid(x = seq(0, 1, .07), y = seq(0, 1, .07))) tgt <- prediction_target(grid = grid, type = "grid")  detect_leakage(d, split = sample(rep_len(1:10, 400)), target = tgt, response = "z",                coords = c("x", "y")) #> <leakage_diagnosis> #>   target            : grid   |  n = 400, test = 400, folds = 10 #>   SLI_rho (signed)  : +0.092   [OPTIMISTIC leakage] #>   SLI_d  (signed)   : +0.034   (A = +0.09289, phi = 2.72) #>   retained corr.    : c_obs = 0.985 vs c_pred = 0.893 #>   W (NNDM) / delta  : 0.09289 / +1.00

title: Leakage channels beyond distance
text: Repeated measurements site, split across folds, leak exactly.
code: dd <- rbind(d, d[sample(400, 60), ])            # 60 duplicated locations gl <- detect_group_leakage(dd, split = sample(rep_len(1:10, nrow(dd))),                            coords = c("x", "y")) gl #> <group_leakage> #>   grouping        : coordinates (tol = 0)  |  n = 460, groups = 400, multi-member = 60 #>   test leaked via shared group : 112 / 460 (24.3%) #>   groups split across folds    : 56 #>   [!] fix: group_kfold() keeps co-located/grouped records together # fix: keep co-located records together gk <- group_kfold(dd, k = 10, coords = c("x", "y")) detect_group_leakage(dd, gk, coords = c("x", "y"))$frac_leaked #> [1] 0

title: Leakage channels beyond distance
text: Test points close training covariate space also easy. Supply deployment covariates get signed index.
code: d$cov <- d$z + rnorm(400, 0, .3) newdata <- data.frame(cov = runif(300, 1.2, 2.2))   # deployment extrapolates the covariate detect_feature_leakage(d, sample(rep_len(1:5, 400)), covariates = "cov", newdata = newdata) #> <feature_leakage> #>   covariates        : cov #>   test in feature-AOA : 91% (interpolation in covariate space) #>   feature SLI (signed): +3.816  [OPTIMISTIC feature leakage] #>   mean NN reach (rel) : test 1.22 vs deployment 5.04

title: Leakage channels beyond distance
text: covariates built focal window radius r, test windows overlap training windows leak. fix buffer 2r.
code: detect_extraction_leakage(d, sample(rep_len(1:5, 400)), radius = 0.08, coords = c("x", "y")) #> <extraction_leakage>  extraction radius = 0.08 #>   test windows overlapping a training window (< 2r): 400 / 400 (100.0%) #>   test windows containing a training location (< r): 400 / 400 (100.0%) #>   [!] fix: separate train/test by a buffer >= 0.16 (= 2 x radius)

title: Leakage channels beyond distance
text: fix temporal leakage forward-chaining CV:
code: st <- d[rep(1:40, each = 10), ]; st$t <- rep(1:10, 40) # random CV trains on the future: detect_temporal_leakage(st, sample(rep_len(1:10, nrow(st))), time = "t") #> <temporal_leakage> #>   time column       : t   |  n test = 400 #>   lookahead leakage : 360 / 400 (90.0%) test points trained on the future #>   mean time-to-nearest-training : 0 #>   [!] fix: temporal_kfold() (forward-chaining CV) # the joint space-time view catches leakage space-only analysis misses: detect_st_leakage(st, sample(rep_len(1:10, nrow(st))), time = "t", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <st_leakage>  (one-step-ahead forecast deployment) #>   space-only SLI : +0.000  [matched] #>   time-only  SLI : +0.370  [optimistic leakage] #>   JOINT space-time SLI : +0.228  [optimistic leakage] #>   median space-time NN: test 0.115 vs deployment 0.370 (in dependence units) fc <- temporal_kfold(st, k = 5, time = "t") detect_temporal_leakage(st, fc, time = "t")$lookahead_frac #> [1] 0

title: Leakage channels beyond distance
text: large-scale trend trend-blind model extrapolate leakage source autocorrelation index see. channels summarised together audit_workflow().
code: trendy <- data.frame(x = runif(200), y = runif(200)) trendy$z <- 3 * trendy$x - 2 * trendy$y + rnorm(200, 0, .2) trend_strength(trendy, "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> [1] 0.9633468

title: Quantify, correct, and design
text: much reported accuracy inflated, relative leakage-controlled scheme? binary (disease-prevalence) count responses, use proper scoring rule:
code: estimate_optimism(d, folds, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <optimism_estimate> #>   metric / control  : RMSE / block #>   user CV error     : 0.2119 #>   controlled error  : 0.7568 #>   optimism          : +0.5449  (+72.0% of controlled)  [OPTIMISTIC (reported accuracy inflated)] db <- d; db$z <- rbinom(400, 1, plogis(2 * sin(2 * pi * d$x))) estimate_optimism(db, folds, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y"), metric = "brier") #> <optimism_estimate> #>   metric / control  : BRIER / block #>   user CV error     : 0.1798 #>   controlled error  : 0.1901 #>   optimism          : +0.0103  (+5.4% of controlled)  [OPTIMISTIC (reported accuracy inflated)]

title: Quantify, correct, and design
text: de-leaked estimate accuracy report, confidence interval. can even correct published number without refitting.
code: deleak_estimate(d, folds, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y"),                 reported = 0.30, n_boot = 200) #> <deleak_estimate> #>   metric / control     : RMSE / block #>   reported (your CV)   : 0.2119 #>   de-leaked estimate   : 0.7497   (90% CI 0.4034, 1.033) #>   => reported accuracy inflated by +72% (error x3.54) #>   your reported 0.3 -> de-leaked 1.062 (90% CI 0.5523, 1.472)

title: Quantify, correct, and design
text: predict_optimism() uses calibrated emulator (refitting); refuses queries outside area applicability.
code: predict_optimism(d, folds, tgt, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y"), model = "idw") #> Warning: Query is outside the emulator's area of applicability; refusing a #> point estimate. Use estimate_optimism() instead. #> <optimism_prediction> (emulator) #>   model / response : idw / gaussian  [match: model+response] #>   optimism         : NA  [outside AOA: DI 0.54 > 0.38]

title: Quantify, correct, and design
text: recommend_validation() gives design-aware guidance; rank_cv_schemes() ranks candidate schemes well match deployment.
code: recommend_validation(estimand = "prediction", design = "clustered", target = "grid") #> <validation_recommendation> #>   estimand / design / target : prediction / clustered / grid #>   spatial CV appropriate     : YES #>   recommended: #>     - NNDM / kNNDM CV #>     - Spatial block CV #>   avoid: Random k-fold CV (optimistic under spatial autocorrelation) #>   rationale: Conditional predictive skill from a clustered sample for a 'grid' target: match the CV geometry to deployment (NNDM/kNNDM/buffered) so test points are as far from training as prediction points are from the sample. rank_cv_schemes(d, tgt, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <scheme_ranking>  target = 'grid' #>   ranked by deployment match (|SLI_rho| -> 0 is best): #>     * block     SLI_rho +0.018  W 0.0254  [well matched] #>       random    SLI_rho +0.093  W 0.0933  [OPTIMISTIC leakage] #>       buffered  SLI_rho -0.197  W 0.222  [PESSIMISTIC (anti-leakage)] #>   recommended: block

title: Quantify, correct, and design
text: Given budget independent validation points, go estimate true accuracy precisely?
code: design_validation(d, tgt, budget = 25, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <validation_design>  budget = 25, allocation = optimal, placed = 24 #>   estimate true accuracy as a weighted mean of validation errors (weights returned) #>   SE of the accuracy estimate: 25% lower than simple random validation (same budget) #>   distance strata (deployment weight x mean error x points placed): #>  weight mean_error n #>   0.169      0.025 1 #>   0.164      0.069 1 #>   0.169      0.134 3 #>   0.164      0.213 4 #>   0.164      0.306 6 #>   0.169      0.427 9

what: A clustered sample (the canonical leakage regime)
title: Detecting and quantifying spatial leakage with spLeakage
text: map wall--wall unit square:
code: set.seed(3) nc <- 8 centers <- cbind(runif(nc, 0.1, 0.9), runif(nc, 0.1, 0.9)) cl <- sample(nc, 500, replace = TRUE) xy <- centers[cl, ] + cbind(rnorm(500, 0, 0.04), rnorm(500, 0, 0.04)) d <- data.frame(x = pmin(pmax(xy[, 1], 0), 1), y = pmin(pmax(xy[, 2], 0), 1)) d$z <- sin(2 * pi * d$x) + cos(2 * pi * d$y) + rnorm(500, 0, 0.15) s <- seq(0.025, 0.975, length.out = 20) tgt <- prediction_target(grid = as.matrix(expand.grid(x = s, y = s)), type = "grid")

what: 1. Detect leakage in a random 80/20-style 10-fold split
title: Detecting and quantifying spatial leakage with spLeakage
text: positive SLI_rho whose interval excludes zero means split optimistically leaking. plot(lk, = "map") shows .
code: folds <- sample(rep_len(1:10, nrow(d))) lk <- detect_leakage(d, split = folds, target = tgt, response = "z",                      coords = c("x", "y"), n_boot = 300) lk #> <leakage_diagnosis> #>   target            : grid   |  n = 500, test = 500, folds = 10 #>   SLI_rho (signed)  : +0.138   [OPTIMISTIC leakage]  90% CI [+0.106, +0.185] #>   SLI_d  (signed)   : +0.053   (A = +0.1539, phi = 2.883)  90% CI [+0.040, +0.076] #>   retained corr.    : c_obs = 0.990 vs c_pred = 0.852 #>   W (NNDM) / delta  : 0.1539 / +1.00 plot(lk)          # observed (CV) vs target (deployment) NN-distance ECDFs

title: Detecting and quantifying spatial leakage with spLeakage
text: reported (random-CV) error compared leakage-controlled, design-matched scheme. gap optimism — large positive inflation.
code: opt <- estimate_optimism(d, split = folds, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y"),                          control = "block") opt #> <optimism_estimate> #>   metric / control  : RMSE / block #>   user CV error     : 0.1965 #>   controlled error  : 0.4364 #>   optimism          : +0.2399  (+55.0% of controlled)  [OPTIMISTIC (reported accuracy inflated)]

title: Detecting and quantifying spatial leakage with spLeakage
text: Declare estimand design (design inferred coordinates): probability sample advice flips — random CV becomes correct spatial CV -pessimistic:
code: recommend_validation(d, estimand = "prediction", design = "clustered",                      target = "grid", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <validation_recommendation> #>   estimand / design / target : prediction / clustered / grid #>   spatial CV appropriate     : YES #>   recommended: #>     - NNDM / kNNDM CV #>     - Spatial block CV #>   avoid: Random k-fold CV (optimistic under spatial autocorrelation) #>   clustering flag (risk only): NN index = 0.71 (clustered) #>   rationale: Conditional predictive skill from a clustered sample for a 'grid' target: match the CV geometry to deployment (NNDM/kNNDM/buffered) so test points are as far from training as prediction points are from the sample. recommend_validation(estimand = "prediction", design = "probability", target = "grid") #> <validation_recommendation> #>   estimand / design / target : prediction / probability / grid #>   spatial CV appropriate     : NO #>   recommended: #>     - Random CV (unbiased for a probability sample) #>   avoid: Forcing spatial CV (over-pessimistic here) #>   rationale: For a probability sample, random CV gives unbiased predictive skill; spatial CV would be pessimistic.

title: Detecting and quantifying spatial leakage with spLeakage
code: rec <- recommend_validation(d, estimand = "prediction", design = "clustered",                             target = "grid", coords = c("x", "y")) report_leakage(lk, optimism = opt, recommendation = rec) #> ================ spLeakage report ================ #>  Leakage grade : C #> -------------------------------------------------- #> <leakage_diagnosis> #>   target            : grid   |  n = 500, test = 500, folds = 10 #>   SLI_rho (signed)  : +0.138   [OPTIMISTIC leakage]  90% CI [+0.106, +0.185] #>   SLI_d  (signed)   : +0.053   (A = +0.1539, phi = 2.883)  90% CI [+0.040, +0.076] #>   retained corr.    : c_obs = 0.990 vs c_pred = 0.852 #>   W (NNDM) / delta  : 0.1539 / +1.00 #>  #> <optimism_estimate> #>   metric / control  : RMSE / block #>   user CV error     : 0.1965 #>   controlled error  : 0.4364 #>   optimism          : +0.2399  (+55.0% of controlled)  [OPTIMISTIC (reported accuracy inflated)] #>  #> <validation_recommendation> #>   estimand / design / target : prediction / clustered / grid #>   spatial CV appropriate     : YES #>   recommended: #>     - NNDM / kNNDM CV #>     - Spatial block CV #>   avoid: Random k-fold CV (optimistic under spatial autocorrelation) #>   clustering flag (risk only): NN index = 0.71 (clustered) #>   rationale: Conditional predictive skill from a clustered sample for a 'grid' target: match the CV geometry to deployment (NNDM/kNNDM/buffered) so test points are as far from training as prediction points are from the sample. #> ==================================================

title: Authors and Citation
text: Olatunji Johnson. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Johnson O (2026). spLeakage: Detect Quantify Spatial Information Leakage Predictive Modelling. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/olatunjijohnson/spLeakage.
code: @Manual{,   title = {spLeakage: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling},   author = {Olatunji Johnson},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/olatunjijohnson/spLeakage}, }

what: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling
title: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling
text: Detect quantify spatial information leakage predictive modelling. spLeakage diagnostic R package. Given spatial data train/test split (existing modelling workflow), : detects leakage across seven channels — geographic autocorrelation (signed Spatial Leakage Index), grouped/duplicated-location, feature-space, covariate-extraction-overlap, temporal, joint spatiotemporal, large-scale trend; quantifies optimism leakage induces (“accuracy ~26% optimistic”), empirical route fast calibrated emulator (adaptive intervals); corrects — bias-corrected (“de-leaked”) accuracy estimate CI, can even correct published number without refitting; recommends appropriate validation strategy (design-aware, including spatial CV right) ranks candidate schemes; designs place budget independent validation points precise estimate true accuracy. audits rather generates folds, interoperating established spatial cross-validation packages (CAST, blockCV, spatialsample, mlr3spatiotempcv). leakage channel comes paired fix (group_kfold, temporal_kfold, target-matched controls), proper scoring rules (Brier/log-loss/Poisson) make optimism meaningful disease-prevalence count responses.

title: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling
text: R CMD check clean (0/0/0). Tutorials: Getting started, Leakage channels beyond distance, Quantify, correct, design (see package vignettes / website articles). method backed GP optimism theory (SLI near- sufficient statistic true optimism), benchmark NNDM, two real-data validations, 16-dataset meta-audit. See docs/VISION.md, docs/THEORY.md, docs/PAPER.md.

title: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling
text: See package vignette full worked example.
code: library(spLeakage)  # Declare where the model will actually be used (wall-to-wall map): tgt <- prediction_target(grid = pred_grid, type = "grid")  # 1. Diagnose an existing split (signed leakage index + uncertainty): lk  <- detect_leakage(data, split = my_folds, target = tgt,                       response = "y", n_boot = 300) lk            # SLI_rho, 90% CI, NNDM W, verdict plot(lk)      # observed-vs-deployment NN-distance ECDFs  # 2. Quantify the optimism it causes: estimate_optimism(data, split = my_folds, response = "y", control = "block")  # 3. Get a design-aware recommendation (design is declared, not inferred): recommend_validation(data, estimand = "prediction",                      design = "clustered", target = "grid")

title: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling
text: Full reference tutorials package website: https://olatunjijohnson.github.io/spLeakage/ Get started — vignette("spLeakage") Leakage channels beyond distance — vignette("channels") Quantify, correct, design — vignette("quantify-correct")

title: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling
text: spLeakage yet CRAN. Install development version GitHub: pak: functionality optional guarded behind Suggests (e.g. xgboost emulator, gstat variograms, rsample/blockCV/mlr3 adaptors); install needed.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("olatunjijohnson/spLeakage")  # to build the vignettes locally as well: remotes::install_github("olatunjijohnson/spLeakage", build_vignettes = TRUE) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("olatunjijohnson/spLeakage")

title: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage in Predictive Modelling

what: Audit a cross-validation workflow for spatial leakage — audit_workflow
title: Audit a cross-validation workflow for spatial leakage — audit_workflow
text: Runs detect_leakage() adds data-hygiene checks (duplicated coordinates, documented CRS) produce scorecard.

title: Audit a cross-validation workflow for spatial leakage — audit_workflow
code: audit_workflow(   data,   split,   target,   response = NULL,   dependence = NULL,   coords = NULL,   group = NULL )

title: Audit a cross-validation workflow for spatial leakage — audit_workflow
text: data sf object, numeric coordinate matrix, data.frame. split split specification: list(test=, train=), list test-index vectors (k folds), fold-id vector length nrow(data). target prediction_target() (coordinates coercible one). response Name numeric response column, used estimate spatial dependence (required unless dependence supplied). dependence precomputed estimate_dependence() object (optional; overrides response). Carries anisotropy used. coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. group Optional grouping column detect_group_leakage() (defaults grouping coordinates).

title: Audit a cross-validation workflow for spatial leakage — audit_workflow
text: object class workflow_audit.

title: De-leaked (bias-corrected) accuracy estimate — deleak_estimate
text: Reports accuracy model achieve leakage-controlled, design-matched validation – de-leaked estimate practitioners report – fold (cluster) bootstrap confidence interval. Optionally corrects user-supplied reported metric value (e.g. published RMSE) using estimated optimism ratio, without refitting original model.

title: De-leaked (bias-corrected) accuracy estimate — deleak_estimate
code: deleak_estimate(   data,   split,   response,   reported = NULL,   target = NULL,   predict_fun = NULL,   metric = c("rmse", "mae", "brier", "logloss", "poisson"),   control = c("block", "buffer"),   coords = NULL,   dependence = NULL,   k_control = NULL,   buffer = NULL,   n_boot = 500L,   level = 0.9 )

title: De-leaked (bias-corrected) accuracy estimate — deleak_estimate
text: data sf object, numeric coordinate matrix, data.frame. split user's split: list(test=, train=), list test-index vectors, fold-id vector length nrow(data). response Name numeric response column. reported Optional reported metric value correct (e.g. published RMSE model available refitting). de-leaked value reported * (controlled / user-CV) error ratio. target Optional prediction_target(). supplied, controlled scheme target-matched (candidate scheme whose SLI_rho target closest zero) instead fixed spatial block – recommended deployment extrapolation, fixed block -corrects. predict_fun function (train_df, test_df) returning numeric predictions test rows. Defaults inverse-distance weighting coordinates. Custom learners (e.g. wrapping lm/ranger) let optimism model-conditional. metric Scoring rule (lower = better): "rmse" (default) "mae" continuous responses; "brier" "logloss" binary responses (predictions [0, 1]); "poisson" deviance counts. Use proper rule response type optimism meaningful (e.g. Brier/log-loss disease prevalence). control leakage-controlled comparison scheme: "block" (spatial k-means blocks, default) "buffer" (buffer user's folds practical range). coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. dependence Optional estimate_dependence() object (used set buffer control = "buffer"). k_control Number control folds (defaults number user folds). buffer Buffer distance control = "buffer" (defaults practical range dependence). n_boot Number fold-bootstrap resamples interval. level Confidence level interval.

title: De-leaked (bias-corrected) accuracy estimate — deleak_estimate
text: object class deleak_estimate.

title: De-leaked (bias-corrected) accuracy estimate — deleak_estimate
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = runif(150), y = runif(150), z = rnorm(150)) deleak_estimate(d, split = sample(rep_len(1:10, 150)), response = "z",                 coords = c("x", "y"), n_boot = 100) #> <deleak_estimate> #>   metric / control     : RMSE / block #>   reported (your CV)   : 1.085 #>   de-leaked estimate   : 1.012   (90% CI 0.8921, 1.103) #>   => reported accuracy inflated by -7% (error x0.93)

what: Design an optimal independent validation sample — design_validation
title: Design an optimal independent validation sample — design_validation
text: Given training locations deployment target, choose place budget independent validation points resulting estimate true map accuracy unbiased (distance--training distribution matches deployment) minimum-variance (distance-stratified Neyman allocation using GP error model).

title: Design an optimal independent validation sample — design_validation
code: design_validation(   data,   target,   budget,   response = NULL,   coords = NULL,   candidates = NULL,   allocation = c("optimal", "proportional", "random"),   strata = 6L,   dependence = NULL,   seed = 1L )

title: Design an optimal independent validation sample — design_validation
text: data Training data (sf, matrix, data.frame). target prediction_target() (deployment region). budget Number validation points place. response Name response column (dependence/error model). coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. candidates Optional pool candidate validation locations (default: target locations). allocation "optimal" (Neyman, default), "proportional", "random". strata Number distance--training strata. dependence Optional estimate_dependence() object. seed RNG seed.

title: Design an optimal independent validation sample — design_validation
text: object class validation_design.

title: Design an optimal independent validation sample — design_validation
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = runif(80), y = runif(80), z = rnorm(80)) grid <- as.matrix(expand.grid(x = seq(0, 1, .1), y = seq(0, 1, .1))) design_validation(d, prediction_target(grid = grid, type = "grid"),                   budget = 20, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <validation_design>  budget = 20, allocation = optimal, placed = 18 #>   estimate true accuracy as a weighted mean of validation errors (weights returned) #>   SE of the accuracy estimate: 0% lower than simple random validation (same budget) #>   distance strata (deployment weight x mean error x points placed): #>  weight mean_error n #>   0.174          1 3 #>   0.165          1 3 #>   0.165          1 3 #>   0.165          1 3 #>   0.157          1 3 #>   0.174          1 3

title: Detect covariate-extraction-overlap leakage — detect_extraction_leakage
text: Flags test points whose covariate-extraction window overlaps training point's, given extraction radius (focal-window radius, buffer distance, kernel bandwidth) used build spatial covariates.

title: Detect covariate-extraction-overlap leakage — detect_extraction_leakage
code: detect_extraction_leakage(data, split, radius, coords = NULL)

title: Detect covariate-extraction-overlap leakage — detect_extraction_leakage
text: data sf object, numeric matrix, data.frame. split split specification (see detect_leakage()). radius extraction-window radius / buffer / bandwidth, coordinate units (geodesic metres geographic CRS). coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices.

title: Detect covariate-extraction-overlap leakage — detect_extraction_leakage
text: object class extraction_leakage.

title: Detect covariate-extraction-overlap leakage — detect_extraction_leakage
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = runif(100), y = runif(100), z = rnorm(100)) # covariates were extracted with a 0.1-unit focal window: detect_extraction_leakage(d, split = sample(rep_len(1:5, 100)),                           radius = 0.1, coords = c("x", "y")) #> <extraction_leakage>  extraction radius = 0.1 #>   test windows overlapping a training window (< 2r): 99 / 100 (99.0%) #>   test windows containing a training location (< r): 91 / 100 (91.0%) #>   [!] fix: separate train/test by a buffer >= 0.2 (= 2 x radius)

title: Detect feature-space (covariate) leakage — detect_feature_leakage
text: Compares close test points training points standardised covariate space close deployment locations sample. positive index means test set easier (closer feature space) deployment – optimistic feature-space leakage / hidden extrapolation deployment.

title: Detect feature-space (covariate) leakage — detect_feature_leakage
code: detect_feature_leakage(data, split, covariates, newdata = NULL, coords = NULL)

title: Detect feature-space (covariate) leakage — detect_feature_leakage
text: data sf object data.frame containing covariate columns. split split specification (see detect_leakage()). covariates Character vector covariate column names. newdata Optional deployment data covariate columns. Without , test-set area--applicability reported (signed index). coords Unused index (kept API symmetry).

title: Detect feature-space (covariate) leakage — detect_feature_leakage
text: object class feature_leakage.

what: Detect grouped / duplicated-location leakage in a split — detect_group_leakage
title: Detect grouped / duplicated-location leakage in a split — detect_group_leakage
text: Flags test observations share group (e.g. site, repeated survey, household, plot) training observation fold – exact leakage distance-based detect_leakage() index registers distance zero.

title: Detect grouped / duplicated-location leakage in a split — detect_group_leakage
code: detect_group_leakage(data, split, group = NULL, coords = NULL, tol = 0)

title: Detect grouped / duplicated-location leakage in a split — detect_group_leakage
text: data sf object, numeric matrix, data.frame. split split specification (see detect_leakage()). group Optional name grouping column. omitted, groups derived coordinates. coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. tol grouping coordinates, distance within points treated location (0 = exact duplicates).

title: Detect grouped / duplicated-location leakage in a split — detect_group_leakage
text: object class group_leakage.

title: Detect grouped / duplicated-location leakage in a split — detect_group_leakage
code: # Two sites, each measured twice; a random split co-locates train and test. d <- data.frame(x = c(1, 1, 2, 2), y = c(1, 1, 2, 2), z = rnorm(4)) detect_group_leakage(d, split = c(1, 2, 1, 2), coords = c("x", "y")) #> <group_leakage> #>   grouping        : coordinates (tol = 0)  |  n = 4, groups = 2, multi-member = 2 #>   test leaked via shared group : 4 / 4 (100.0%) #>   groups split across folds    : 2 #>   [!] fix: group_kfold() keeps co-located/grouped records together

what: Detect and quantify spatial leakage in a train/test split — detect_leakage
title: Detect and quantify spatial leakage in a train/test split — detect_leakage
text: Audits existing split cross-validation scheme: computes Spatial Leakage Index (signed) model-free distance form (SLI_d) covariance-aware dependence form (SLI_rho), per-point leakage decomposition. Positive values indicate optimistic leakage (CV easier deployment); negative values indicate pessimism. See docs/METHOD-SLI.md.

title: Detect and quantify spatial leakage in a train/test split — detect_leakage
code: detect_leakage(   data,   split,   target,   response = NULL,   dependence = NULL,   coords = NULL,   n_boot = 0L,   k = 1L )

title: Detect and quantify spatial leakage in a train/test split — detect_leakage
text: data sf object, numeric coordinate matrix, data.frame. split split specification: list(test=, train=), list test-index vectors (k folds), fold-id vector length nrow(data). target prediction_target() (coordinates coercible one). response Name numeric response column, used estimate spatial dependence (required unless dependence supplied). dependence precomputed estimate_dependence() object (optional; overrides response). Carries anisotropy used. coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. n_boot Number Monte-Carlo draws variogram-fit uncertainty used put confidence interval SLI. 0 (default) skips . split geometry exact, rho/phi uncertainty propagated. k Number nearest training neighbours dependence-form SLI_rho (density-aware, noisy-retained correlation). 1 (default, recommended) single-nearest-neighbour index, near-sufficient predicting optimism (cor ~ 0.98 exact GP optimism). k > 1 improves magnitude match reduces correlation (-counts correlated neighbours), offered option rather default.

title: Detect and quantify spatial leakage in a train/test split — detect_leakage
text: object class leakage_diagnosis.

title: Detect and quantify spatial leakage in a train/test split — detect_leakage
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = runif(80), y = runif(80), z = rnorm(80)) folds <- sample(rep_len(1:5, 80)) grid <- as.matrix(expand.grid(x = seq(0, 1, 0.2), y = seq(0, 1, 0.2))) tgt <- prediction_target(grid = grid, type = "grid") detect_leakage(d, folds, tgt, response = "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <leakage_diagnosis> #>   target            : grid   |  n = 80, test = 80, folds = 5 #>   SLI_rho (signed)  : +0.000   [well matched] #>   SLI_d  (signed)   : +0.026   (A = +0.005592, phi = 0.2176) #>   retained corr.    : c_obs = 0.000 vs c_pred = 0.000 #>   W (NNDM) / delta  : 0.007047 / +0.79

title: Detect joint spatiotemporal leakage — detect_st_leakage
text: Measures leakage scaled space-time metric (axis divided dependence range) split, relative deployment target (default one-step-ahead forecast). Reports joint space-time index together space-time-indices, since joint measure can differ either.

title: Detect joint spatiotemporal leakage — detect_st_leakage
code: detect_st_leakage(   data,   split,   time,   coords = NULL,   sp_range = NULL,   t_range = NULL,   horizon = NULL )

title: Detect joint spatiotemporal leakage — detect_st_leakage
text: data sf object, numeric matrix, data.frame. split split specification (see detect_leakage()). time Name time column (numeric Date). coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. sp_range, t_range Spatial / temporal dependence ranges used scale two axes (defaults: 30% spatial extent / temporal span). Supply estimated ranges calibrated metric. horizon Forecast horizon default deployment target (defaults median temporal spacing).

title: Detect joint spatiotemporal leakage — detect_st_leakage
text: object class st_leakage.

what: Detect temporal (lookahead) leakage in a split — detect_temporal_leakage
title: Detect temporal (lookahead) leakage in a split — detect_temporal_leakage
text: Flags test observations whose fold's training set contains later time points (model trained future predict past) summarises close time test points training data.

title: Detect temporal (lookahead) leakage in a split — detect_temporal_leakage
code: detect_temporal_leakage(data, split, time, coords = NULL)

title: Detect temporal (lookahead) leakage in a split — detect_temporal_leakage
text: data sf object data.frame. split split specification (see detect_leakage()). time Name time column (numeric Date). coords Unused (kept API symmetry).

title: Detect temporal (lookahead) leakage in a split — detect_temporal_leakage
text: object class temporal_leakage.

what: Estimate spatial dependence (variogram, range, correlation, anisotropy) — estimate_dependence
title: Estimate spatial dependence (variogram, range, correlation, anisotropy) — estimate_dependence
text: Fits empirical semivariogram exponential model, returning correlation function rho(h), variogram range, practical range phi, optional geometric anisotropy, parameter covariance used propagate uncertainty Spatial Leakage Index.

title: Estimate spatial dependence (variogram, range, correlation, anisotropy) — estimate_dependence
code: estimate_dependence(   data,   response,   coords = NULL,   n_bins = 15L,   cutoff = NULL,   anisotropy = NULL )

title: Estimate spatial dependence (variogram, range, correlation, anisotropy) — estimate_dependence
text: data sf object, numeric coordinate matrix, data.frame. response Name (numeric) response column used variogram. coords non-sf input, column names/indices coordinates. n_bins Number variogram bins. cutoff Maximum lag distance; defaults half maximum pairwise distance. anisotropy NULL (isotropic, default), "auto" (estimate geometric anisotropy directional variograms; projected data ), list(angle =, ratio =) giving major-axis angle (radians) minor/major range ratio (0, 1].

title: Estimate spatial dependence (variogram, range, correlation, anisotropy) — estimate_dependence
text: object class sp_dependence elements rho (function), range, practical_range (phi), psill, nugget, signal_prop, anisotropy (NULL), coef, vcov, variogram.

what: Estimate the optimism (accuracy inflation) of a cross-validation scheme — estimate_optimism
title: Estimate the optimism (accuracy inflation) of a cross-validation scheme — estimate_optimism
text: Re-evaluates model user's split leakage-controlled scheme, reports gap optimism: much user's reported accuracy inflated. Positive optimism means user's CV easy (optimistic); negative means pessimistic (e.g. -blocked) – reported faithfully.

title: Estimate the optimism (accuracy inflation) of a cross-validation scheme — estimate_optimism
code: estimate_optimism(   data,   split,   response,   predict_fun = NULL,   coords = NULL,   metric = c("rmse", "mae", "brier", "logloss", "poisson"),   control = c("block", "buffer"),   dependence = NULL,   k_control = NULL,   buffer = NULL )

title: Estimate the optimism (accuracy inflation) of a cross-validation scheme — estimate_optimism
text: data sf object, numeric coordinate matrix, data.frame. split user's split: list(test=, train=), list test-index vectors, fold-id vector length nrow(data). response Name numeric response column. predict_fun function (train_df, test_df) returning numeric predictions test rows. Defaults inverse-distance weighting coordinates. Custom learners (e.g. wrapping lm/ranger) let optimism model-conditional. coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. metric Scoring rule (lower = better): "rmse" (default) "mae" continuous responses; "brier" "logloss" binary responses (predictions [0, 1]); "poisson" deviance counts. Use proper rule response type optimism meaningful (e.g. Brier/log-loss disease prevalence). control leakage-controlled comparison scheme: "block" (spatial k-means blocks, default) "buffer" (buffer user's folds practical range). dependence Optional estimate_dependence() object (used set buffer control = "buffer"). k_control Number control folds (defaults number user folds). buffer Buffer distance control = "buffer" (defaults practical range dependence).

title: Estimate the optimism (accuracy inflation) of a cross-validation scheme — estimate_optimism
text: object class optimism_estimate.

title: Estimate the optimism (accuracy inflation) of a cross-validation scheme — estimate_optimism
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = runif(120), y = runif(120), z = rnorm(120)) estimate_optimism(d, split = sample(rep_len(1:10, 120)), response = "z",                   coords = c("x", "y")) #> <optimism_estimate> #>   metric / control  : RMSE / block #>   user CV error     : 1.028 #>   controlled error  : 1.039 #>   optimism          : +0.01121  (+1.1% of controlled)  [OPTIMISTIC (reported accuracy inflated)]

what: Group-aware k-fold assignment (the fix for grouped leakage) — group_kfold
title: Group-aware k-fold assignment (the fix for grouped leakage) — group_kfold
text: Assigns whole groups folds members group (e.g. co-located records) never split across train test. remedy leakage detect_group_leakage() flags.

title: Group-aware k-fold assignment (the fix for grouped leakage) — group_kfold
code: group_kfold(data, k = 10L, group = NULL, coords = NULL, tol = 0)

title: Group-aware k-fold assignment (the fix for grouped leakage) — group_kfold
text: data sf object, numeric matrix, data.frame. k Number folds. group Optional name grouping column. omitted, groups derived coordinates. coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. tol grouping coordinates, distance within points treated location (0 = exact duplicates).

title: Group-aware k-fold assignment (the fix for grouped leakage) — group_kfold
text: integer fold-id vector length nrow(data).

text: Plot leakage diagnosis

title: Plot a leakage diagnosis — plot.leakage_diagnosis
text: x leakage_diagnosis object. "ecdf" (observed vs target NN-distance ECDFs) "map" (per-point leakage contribution space). ... Passed underlying plot call.

title: Plot a leakage diagnosis — plot.leakage_diagnosis
text: x, invisibly.

what: Predict optimism from the calibrated emulator (fast, no model refitting) — predict_optimism
title: Predict optimism from the calibrated emulator (fast, no model refitting) — predict_optimism
text: Estimates optimism split using pre-trained emulator cheap data/split/target features – fast counterpart estimate_optimism(). estimate conditioned model class response type. query falls outside emulator's area applicability (feature category space) function refuses point estimate (NA) rather extrapolating.

title: Predict optimism from the calibrated emulator (fast, no model refitting) — predict_optimism
code: predict_optimism(   data,   split,   target,   response = NULL,   model = c("idw", "rf", "gam"),   response_type = c("auto", "gaussian", "poisson", "binomial"),   dependence = NULL,   coords = NULL,   emulator = NULL )

title: Predict optimism from the calibrated emulator (fast, no model refitting) — predict_optimism
text: data sf object, numeric coordinate matrix, data.frame. split split specification: list(test=, train=), list test-index vectors (k folds), fold-id vector length nrow(data). target prediction_target() (coordinates coercible one). response Name numeric response column, used estimate spatial dependence (required unless dependence supplied). model learner whose optimism estimate: "idw" (default), "rf", "gam". response_type "auto" (detect response, default), "gaussian", "poisson", "binomial". dependence precomputed estimate_dependence() object (optional; overrides response). Carries anisotropy used. coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. emulator optimism_emulator; defaults one shipped package.

title: Predict optimism from the calibrated emulator (fast, no model refitting) — predict_optimism
text: object class optimism_prediction.

what: Declare the prediction target (deployment geometry) — prediction_target
title: Declare the prediction target (deployment geometry) — prediction_target
text: Spatial Leakage Index defined relative model actually used. constructor declares deployment geometry.

title: Declare the prediction target (deployment geometry) — prediction_target
code: prediction_target(   data = NULL,   grid = NULL,   newdata = NULL,   type = c("grid", "newdata", "interpolation"),   coords = NULL,   n = 5000L )

title: Declare the prediction target (deployment geometry) — prediction_target
text: data Sample data (used derive interpolation grid, CRS). grid, newdata Explicit prediction locations (sf, matrix, data.frame) type = "grid" / "newdata". type One "grid" (wall--wall mapping), "newdata" (supplied locations), "interpolation" (unsampled locations within sampled domain, generated data). coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. n Number points generate type = "interpolation".

title: Declare the prediction target (deployment geometry) — prediction_target
text: object class prediction_target holding prediction coordinates geometry info.

what: Rank candidate cross-validation schemes by deployment match — rank_cv_schemes
title: Rank candidate cross-validation schemes by deployment match — rank_cv_schemes
text: Builds candidate CV scheme, measures Spatial Leakage Index declared prediction target, ranks : scheme whose SLI_rho closest zero best imitates deployment (neither optimistic pessimistic).

title: Rank candidate cross-validation schemes by deployment match — rank_cv_schemes
code: rank_cv_schemes(   data,   target,   response,   coords = NULL,   k = 10L,   schemes = c("random", "block", "buffered"),   dependence = NULL,   seed = 1L )

title: Rank candidate cross-validation schemes by deployment match — rank_cv_schemes
text: data sf object, numeric matrix, data.frame. target prediction_target() (coordinates coercible one). response Name numeric response column (dependence model). coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. k Number folds candidate scheme. schemes Candidate schemes: "random", "block", "buffered". dependence Optional precomputed estimate_dependence() object. seed RNG seed random scheme.

title: Rank candidate cross-validation schemes by deployment match — rank_cv_schemes
text: object class scheme_ranking.

title: Rank candidate cross-validation schemes by deployment match — rank_cv_schemes
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = runif(150), y = runif(150), z = rnorm(150)) grid <- as.matrix(expand.grid(x = seq(0, 1, .2), y = seq(0, 1, .2))) rank_cv_schemes(d, prediction_target(grid = grid, type = "grid"),                 response = "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> <scheme_ranking>  target = 'grid' #>   ranked by deployment match (|SLI_rho| -> 0 is best): #>     * random    SLI_rho +0.001  W 0.0124  [well matched] #>       block     SLI_rho -0.009  W 0.0735  [well matched] #>       buffered  SLI_rho -0.031  W 0.359  [PESSIMISTIC (anti-leakage)] #>   recommended: random

what: Recommend a validation strategy for a sampling design and prediction target — recommend_validation
title: Recommend a validation strategy for a sampling design and prediction target — recommend_validation
text: Operationalises design x estimand x target framework: asks estimand sampling design (inferred coordinates) returns ranked, conditional recommendation – including spatial CV appropriate. supplied data used compute clustering risk flag, never infer design.

title: Recommend a validation strategy for a sampling design and prediction target — recommend_validation
code: recommend_validation(   data = NULL,   estimand = c("prediction", "population"),   design = c("unknown", "probability", "clustered", "convenience"),   target = c("grid", "interpolation", "newdata"),   coords = NULL )

title: Recommend a validation strategy for a sampling design and prediction target — recommend_validation
text: data Optional sf/matrix/data.frame, used clustering flag. estimand "prediction" (conditional predictive skill locations, default) "population" (population-mean map accuracy region). design "unknown" (default), "probability", "clustered", "convenience". elicited fact data collection. target "grid" (wall--wall, default), "interpolation", "newdata". coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices.

title: Recommend a validation strategy for a sampling design and prediction target — recommend_validation
text: object class validation_recommendation.

title: Recommend a validation strategy for a sampling design and prediction target — recommend_validation
code: # A clustered sample to be mapped wall-to-wall: spatial CV is appropriate. recommend_validation(estimand = "prediction", design = "clustered", target = "grid") #> <validation_recommendation> #>   estimand / design / target : prediction / clustered / grid #>   spatial CV appropriate     : YES #>   recommended: #>     - NNDM / kNNDM CV #>     - Spatial block CV #>   avoid: Random k-fold CV (optimistic under spatial autocorrelation) #>   rationale: Conditional predictive skill from a clustered sample for a 'grid' target: match the CV geometry to deployment (NNDM/kNNDM/buffered) so test points are as far from training as prediction points are from the sample. # A probability sample: random CV is correct, spatial CV is over-pessimistic. recommend_validation(estimand = "prediction", design = "probability", target = "grid") #> <validation_recommendation> #>   estimand / design / target : prediction / probability / grid #>   spatial CV appropriate     : NO #>   recommended: #>     - Random CV (unbiased for a probability sample) #>   avoid: Forcing spatial CV (over-pessimistic here) #>   rationale: For a probability sample, random CV gives unbiased predictive skill; spatial CV would be pessimistic.

text: diagnosis detect_leakage() result. optimism optional estimate_optimism() result. recommendation optional recommend_validation() result. deleak optional deleak_estimate() result (corrected accuracy).

what: spLeakage: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage — spLeakage-package
title: spLeakage: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage — spLeakage-package
text: Diagnostic tools spatial information leakage predictive modelling. spLeakage audits train/test split modelling workflow, quantifies leakage optimism induces, recommends appropriate validation strategy user's sampling design prediction target.

title: spLeakage: Detect and Quantify Spatial Information Leakage — spLeakage-package
text: Maintainer: Olatunji Johnson teejaystatistics@gmail.com

title: Forward-chaining temporal cross-validation (the fix for lookahead leakage) — temporal_kfold

what: Trend strength: large-scale structure that resists extrapolation — trend_strength
title: Trend strength: large-scale structure that resists extrapolation — trend_strength
text: proportion response variance explained low-order polynomial coordinates – measure large-scale spatial trend. High trend strength means trend-blind model extrapolate poorly, leakage channel autocorrelation detect_leakage() index capture.

title: Trend strength: large-scale structure that resists extrapolation — trend_strength
code: trend_strength(data, response, coords = NULL, degree = 2L)

title: Trend strength: large-scale structure that resists extrapolation — trend_strength
text: data sf object, numeric matrix, data.frame. response Name numeric response column. coords non-sf input, coordinate column names/indices. degree Polynomial degree coordinate trend surface (default 2).

title: Trend strength: large-scale structure that resists extrapolation — trend_strength
text: single number [0, 1]: trend R^2.

title: Trend strength: large-scale structure that resists extrapolation — trend_strength
code: set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = runif(100), y = runif(100)) d$z <- 3 * d$x + rnorm(100, 0, 0.2)        # strong x-trend trend_strength(d, "z", coords = c("x", "y")) #> [1] 0.9466331

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: First release: diagnostic toolkit spatial information leakage.

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: detect_leakage() — signed Spatial Leakage Index (SLI_rho dependence form SLI_d distance form), crossing decomposition, per-point/per-fold leakage map, geometric anisotropy, Monte-Carlo uncertainty (n_boot), optional multi-neighbour retained-correlation form (k; k = 1 default recommended). estimate_dependence() — variogram, correlation function, practical range, optional anisotropy.

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: estimate_optimism() — empirical route: optimism relative design-matched leakage-controlled scheme (can negative probability samples). Supports proper scoring rules (brier/logloss binary, poisson deviance counts) optimism meaningful disease-prevalence count responses. predict_optimism() — fast emulator route (gradient-boosted point estimate + normalized split-conformal intervals widen harder regions) area--applicability guard. Calibrated simulation study (data-raw/simulate_optimism.R). deleak_estimate() — bias-corrected (“de-leaked”) accuracy estimate fold bootstrap CI; can also correct published metric value without refitting, (target=) uses target-matched control extrapolation deployment.

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: recommend_validation() — estimand-first, design × target decision engine; design elicited, never inferred geometry. rank_cv_schemes() — data-driven: ranks candidate CV schemes well geometry matches declared deployment target (smallest |SLI_rho|). audit_workflow(), report_leakage() — submission-ready scorecard.

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: detect_group_leakage() / group_kfold() — grouped / duplicated-location leakage. detect_feature_leakage() — covariate-space (area--applicability) leakage. detect_extraction_leakage() — covariate-extraction-overlap leakage (focal/buffer/ kernel windows straddle train/test boundary; common remote sensing/SDM). detect_temporal_leakage() / temporal_kfold() — temporal lookahead leakage. detect_st_leakage() — joint spatiotemporal leakage scaled space-time metric (catches leakage space-time-analysis misses). trend_strength() — large-scale trend channel (extrapolation-optimism trend-blind model misses; second optimism source found meta-audit). Surfaced audit_workflow().

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: design_validation() — optimal placement budget independent validation points (distance-stratified Neyman allocation + inclusion weights) estimate true map accuracy minimum variance (~45% lower SD random placement tests).

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: Accepts plain fold vectors, fold lists, list(test=, train=), pre-built fold lists, tidymodels rsample rset objects.

title: spLeakage 0.1.0
text: End--end real-data case study Nigeria malaria prevalence (docs/CASE-STUDY-NIGERIA.md).
